nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; adults have been found singly or in small to large aggregations in passes and on steep outer reef slopes. Recorded at depths of approximately 3–40 m in tropical Indo-Pacific waters.

decorPreferences: steep rockwork slopes mimicking outer reef

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild, feeding on zooplankton above reef structures (FishBase trophic level 3.4). In captivity tends to accept a varied diet of frozen meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, and finely chopped seafood, alongside marine flake and micro-pellets. Multiple small feedings per day are generally advised to mirror natural midwater plankton feeding.

avoidWith: large Pomacentrid damselfish in small tanks

summary: A peaceful, schooling damselfish from the Indo-Pacific, typically reaching around 13.5 cm. Generally considered reef-safe and well-suited to groups of three or more in established reef aquariums with open swimming space.
careNotes:
Weber's Chromis (Chromis weberi) is a mid-sized Indo-Pacific damselfish in the family Pomacentridae, described by Fowler & Bean in 1928. Its range spans the Red Sea and East Africa east to the Line Islands, north to southern Japan, and south to New Caledonia — one of the broader distributions in the genus.

In the wild it tends to form small to large aggregations in passes and on steep outer reef slopes at roughly 3–40 m depth, where groups feed on zooplankton in the water column. This active midwater swimming habit means it benefits from a tank with generous open water; a 120 cm footprint is generally advised as a practical minimum for a small group.

Adult size: FishBase records a maximum of 13.5 cm TL, making this one of the larger common Chromis species in the hobby (compare: Chromis viridis ~6 cm, Chromis nitida ~10 cm). A size range of approximately 10–13.5 cm is used here to reflect typical captive adult size.

Temperature: FishBase reports a preferred range of 25.8–29°C (mean 28°C), slightly warmer than some other Chromis species. Standard reef temperatures of 25–26°C are generally workable; maintaining the upper end of this range (~26–28°C) is thought to better match wild conditions.

Grouping: LiveAquaria's chromis collection guidance notes that chromis enjoy being kept in schools of three or more. Conspecific hierarchies have been documented in captive groups, with dominant individuals sometimes harassing subordinates in undersized tanks — larger groups in larger systems tend to diffuse this.

Reef safety: The species is generally considered reef-safe. As a planktivore it has no significant tendency to nip corals or bother invertebrates.

Care level: LiveAquaria classifies chromis as easy, consistent with the hardy reputation of the genus. This species is rated beginner here; it is undemanding about water parameters within normal reef ranges and typically accepts prepared foods readily.

Reproduction: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding at breeding. Eggs are demersal and adhere to the substrate; males guard and aerate the clutch. Captive breeding would require conditions similar to other Chromis species — a suitable flat spawning surface and protection of eggs from tankmates.

Note: LiveAquaria's individual product page for this species was unavailable during research (out of stock / page not found). Care data were supplemented from the LiveAquaria chromis collection page and FishBase species summary. The minimum-confirmation bar was met via FishBase (primary biological data) and LiveAquaria's chromis category guidance.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding. Eggs are demersal and adhere to the substrate; males guard and aerate the clutch. Captive spawning has not been widely documented for this species specifically, and rearing the small pelagic larvae would generally be considered difficult.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: LiveAquaria chromis collection page. Individual product page for Chromis weberi returned 404 (out of stock). Collection page confirmed: care level easy, reef-compatible, schooling (3+). LiveAquaria is the designated primary for fish-saltwater slice.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pomacentridae, Ovalentaria/misc), max length 13.5 cm TL, native range (Indo-Pacific: Red Sea and East Africa to Line Islands, north to Japan, south to New Caledonia), preferred temperature 25.8–29°C (mean 28°C), depth 3–40 m, habitat (outer reef slopes and passes, singly or in aggregations), trophic level 3.4, diurnal activity, oviparous reproduction with male egg-guarding. IUCN Least Concern. Salinity and pH not explicitly stated by FishBase for this species; standard marine reef values applied.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; distinct pair bonding at breeding. Demersal adhesive eggs guarded and aerated by the male. Captive spawning not widely documented; rearing small pelagic larvae considered difficult.
